data.title: Analysts eye bright FY26 outlook for Oracle on cloud, RPO growth
data.description:
Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) appears to be taking advantage of the artificial intelligence build out, as its cloud revenue is expected to surge 40% year over year in fiscal 2026, which doesn't even include its role in the Stargate Project.

Shares jumped 8% by early trading Thursday, following the software and cloud infrastructure company's fourth quarter fiscal 2025 results and outlook.

Most analysts raised their price target on the stock, highlighting strong cloud growth and high remaining performance obligations.

"Total RPO is expected to grow >100% in FY26 vs. 41% in FY25 driven by robust demand in both AI and non-AI workloads," said Jefferies analysts, led by brent Thill, in a Thursday note. "Mgmt emphasized the sustained momentum in its infrastructure services, underpinned by large, non-cancelable contracts within the RPO, which bolsters confidence in continued OCI revenue growth. ORCL highlighted that they can meet its 100% RPO growth target w/o Stargate, and any success from the venture would be incremental upside."

Jefferies retained its Buy rating and increased its price target to $220 from $200.

Bank of America Securities hiked its price target to $220 from $156 and reiterated its Neutral rating.

"Clearly, the pipeline for OCI and AI deals is trending well," said BofA analysts Brad Sills and Carly Liu. "What is not clear is the trajectory of profitability, with ramping capex likely to weigh on gross margin from here. While scale on operating expenses provides some offset, the economics in Oracle's business are in transition."

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley maintained its $175 price target and Equal-weight rating, but said if Oracle's expectations come to fruition, then the stock is undervalued.

"Oracle Cloud Infrastructure is expected to accelerate from 50% to >70% YoY, and overall Cloud revenue is expected to accelerate from 24% to >40% in FY26," said Morgan Stanley analysts Keith Weiss and Jamie Reynolds, in a note. "If these targets prove realistic, Oracle will end FY26 with close to $280 billion in backlog, assuming roughly ⅓ of this is current, they will have almost 90% of their $104 billion FY29 target already in backlog."

Oracle co-founder and chief technology officer Lawrence Ellison highlighted the value of his company's vast database in training AI models during Wednesday night's earnings call.

"This is why our database business is going to grow dramatically," Ellison said. "Think about it. You have to put all of your data into a database. So that database must be highly secure. It must be scalable. It must be economical. It must be reliable, 7 days a week, 24 hours a day. It has to be fail tolerant. It can never break. That's how Oracle got popular in the first place. But then it has to hold the data in a way that's consumable by the AI models. In other words, the data has to be vectorized and searchable by the AI models, and you use that data to train up those models on your data. Who else is doing that? Let me answer the question, nobody." 

Shares for the big three cloud providers, Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) showed little movement during early Thursday action. Microsoft edged up 0.7%, while Amazon and Google were both down about 0.3%.

data.title: Castellum prices $5M public offering of units; shares down 11%
data.description:
• Castellum (NYSE:CTM) announced the pricing of its public offering of 4.17M units at $1.20/ unit.
  • Each unit consists of one share of common stock and one warrant to purchase one share of common stock.
  • The warrants will be immediately exercisable at $1.22 per share and will expire 60 days from the date of issuance.
  • The shares of common stock and warrants are immediately separable and will be issued separately.
  • Gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be ~$5M.
  • Castellum intends to use the net proceeds of the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • CTM shares fall 11% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Allegion to acquire ELATEC for €330M
data.description:
• Allegion (NYSE:ALLE) has signed a definitive agreement to acquire ELATEC, including Elatec GmbH and other group entities, ("ELATEC") from Summit Partners for €330M on a cash-free, debt-free basis.
  • Allegion expects to fund the acquisition through a combination of cash on hand and borrowings under its existing revolving credit facility.
  • The company expects ELATEC to generate around €60-€65M in net sales in 2026 and be accretive to 2026 adjusted earnings per share (EPS).
  • This acquisition is expected to close in the third quarter of 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • ALLE closed -1.23% at $137.94.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AIM ImmunoTech granted U.S. patent for Ampligen
data.description:
• AIM ImmunoTech (OTC:AIMI) said on Thursday that the United States Patent and Trademark Office has granted a U.S. patent, titled “Therapeutic Double Stranded RNA and Methods for Producing the Same.” 
  • The patent expires January 25, 2041.
  • The new patent covers methods involving the manufacture of a range of therapeutic double-stranded RNA products, of which Ampligen is included. 
  • The new patent significantly extends the potential development runway for the drug. AIM’s overall intellectual property portfolio covers the manufacture and use of Ampligen. 
  • AIM’s patents include Ampligen in the treatment of cancer, the Post-COVID condition of fatigue, and endometriosis.

data.title: Inuvo reaffirms Q2 revenue growth view of no less than 25% Y/Y
data.description:
• Inuvo (OTCQB:INVU) has announced that it was reaffirming its prior guidance that the Co. was expecting revenue growth for the second quarter of not less than 25% on a Y/Y basis.
  • The Co. also completed a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its outstanding common stock, as approved by shareholders at the Annual Meeting held on May 22, 2025.
  • Inuvo’s purpose in effectuating the reverse stock split is to improve the marketability and liquidity of its stock, aiming to attract a broader range of institutional investors and analysts in support of its long-term growth strategy.

data.title: Jeffs' Brands announces 1-for-17 reverse stock split; shares down over 30%
data.description:
• Jeffs' Brands (NASDAQ:JFBR) intends to effect a 1-for-17 reverse stock split.
  • The ordinary shares are expected to begin trading on Nasdaq a post-split basis at the opening of trading on June 16, 2025.
  • The reverse share split will reduce the number of outstanding shares from 9.41M shares to ~553,385 shares.
  • JFBR shares tumble over 30% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Sterling Infrastructure announces CFO appointment
data.description:
• Sterling Infrastructure (NASDAQ:STRL) announced the appointment of Nicholas Grindstaff to the role of chief financial officer (CFO), effective July 10, 2025.
  • Grindstaff is succeeding Ron Ballschmiede, who served as executive vice president, CFO from November 2015 through May 2024, and as Interim CFO from March 2025.
  • Grindstaff most recently served as CFO of Cinterra.
  • STRL shares fall 1.5% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release
